translated: I will go and find the small one. I will go and look for it. I will go to Uwumbɔr to look for it. If I find it, I will bring it back to you.

text: Le Juda baa u ke, “Aa ban ke m tii si ba aan ni li ye tipuur?” Le u giin bui u ke, “Di saabɔmbik, ni nkuŋmin, ni saah joo kijaangbeek ki na tii mi.” Le u di ni mɔmɔk tii u. Le bi kpaan, le Tamar dek lipuul.
translated: The Jew asked, “What do you want from me?” He replied, “I want a goat, a sheep, and a cow. I will give you the price.” He agreed and gave him the animals. When the time came, Tamar brought the goat.

text: Le Esɔ bui ke, “U jin mi nfum mulee le na. Mbamɔn, ni ŋeer bi yin u ke Jakob. Njan u nan ŋmann mi ki gaa tininkpir, le ki ki ŋmann tite mu ki gaa maanyoor. Nte, saa gur tinyoor tibaa ki ŋa m pu uu?”

translated: “Where is he, Jakob? I saw him yesterday, he was going to the market, and then he went to Lebanon. I want to know if he is well. I want to know if he is alive. Where is he?”

text: Ti yaa ŋa baah len pu na kan, baawakor ni tiwan nimɔk bi kpa na aan li ye timi yaan aa? Nima pu na, cha ti kii ki gii ŋichakpan man, aan bi li bi ti chee.”
translated: You are saying that you will not come again, is that true? If that is so, then I will not wait for you again, I will go.

text: Le u nan puu tipuur nima chee ki tii Uwumbɔr ke, “Sin Uwumbɔr yaa ga li bi m chee, ki joo mi maasachuln ni, ki ga tii mi tijikaar ni tiwanpeenkaan,
